hooks.text: Your bank says wire transfers to your supplier are free. The same confirmation hides a foreign exchange margin, FX, that quietly took eleven hundred dollars off one payment.
There is one number on your last supplier wire confirmation your bank hopes you never compare to Google. Find it in thirty seconds and you see exactly what they took.
Applied rate 7.05 on your confirmation. The real rate that morning was 7.22. On a fifty thousand dollar supplier wire, that gap is eleven hundred seventy five dollars, gone silently.
A business owner opened an old wire confirmation, googled the rate for that exact day, and did one quick sum. The number staring back was money the bank never mentioned.
Every wire confirmation hides one rate the bank never shows beside it. Skip this thirty second check and you keep paying a foreign exchange, FX, margin on every supplier payment.
